originalTitle: Wall St hits new closing highs on tech strength, Iran deal hopes
summary: STORY: Wall Street's main indexes hit more record closing highs on Friday, with the Dow gaining nearly three-quarters of a percent, and the S&P 500 and Nasdaq each ticking up about two-tenths of a percent. All three indexes posted both weekly and monthly gains, with the S&P 500 recording its ninth straight weekly gain, it's longest streak since December of 2023. Mary Ann Bartels, chief investment strategist at Sanctuary Wealth, said that while there may be some bumps ahead, the market's upward momentum could continue through the end of the decade. "You know, the old adage is, 'Sell in May and go away.' And well, although you may want to take some profits, maybe rebalance, because we may have some volatility as we approach the summer months. So what we're saying is, 'Sell in May, but you may want to come back.' [FLASH] Because we believe that the equity market remains in a secular bull market." Stocks on the move Friday included Dell, which surged nearly 33% after the company raised its full-year profit and revenue forecasts a day earlier. Peers Super Micro Computer and Hewlett Packard Enterprise rose more than 11% and 12%, respectively. On the flip side, consumer staples shares were weak, with heavyweight Costco dropping almost 4% and Walmart shedding roughly 2.5% And the S&P automaker index slipped after reports the Trump administration wants North American-built vehicles to have 82% regional content to qualify for preferential treatment under the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement. Shares of General Motors and U.S.-listed shares of Stellantis both ended lower.

Mary Ann Bartels, chief investment strategist at Sanctuary Wealth, said that while there may be some bumps ahead, the market's upward momentum could continue through the end of the decade. "You know, the old adage is, 'Sell in May and go away.' And well, although you may want to take some profits, maybe rebalance, because we may have some volatility as we approach the summer months. So what we're saying is, 'Sell in May, but you may want to come back.' [FLASH] Because we believe that the equity market remains in a secular bull market." Stocks on the move Friday included Dell, which surged nearly 33% after the company raised its full-year profit and revenue forecasts a day earlier. Peers Super Micro Computer and Hewlett Packard Enterprise rose more than 11% and 12%, respectively. On the flip side, consumer staples shares were weak, with heavyweight Costco dropping almost 4% and Walmart shedding roughly 2.5% And the S&P automaker index slipped after reports the Trump administration wants North American-built vehicles to have 82% regional content to qualify for preferential treatment under the U.S.-Mexico-Canada Agreement. chineseTitle: 华尔街因科技实力和伊朗协议希望而创下收盘新高
chineseSummary: 故事：周五，华尔街主要股指再创新高，道指上涨近四分之三，标准普尔 500 指数和纳斯达克指数均上涨约十分之二。所有三个指数均录得每周和每月上涨，其中标准普尔 500 指数连续第九周上涨，这是自 2023 年 12 月以来最长的连续上涨。 Sanctuary Wealth 首席投资策略师玛丽·安·巴特尔斯 (Mary Ann Bartels) 表示，尽管未来可能会出现一些坎坷，但市场的上涨势头可能会持续到本世纪末。 “你知道，古老的格言是，‘五月卖出然后走开。’好吧，尽管您可能想获利了结，但也许需要重新平衡，因为随着夏季的临近，我们可能会出现一些波动。所以我们想说的是，“五月出售，但你可能想回来。” [FLASH]因为我们相信股市仍处于长期牛市中。”周五上涨的股票包括戴尔，该公司前一天上调全年利润和收入预测后，该公司股价飙升近 33%。同行超微电脑和惠普企业分别上涨超过11%和12%。另一方面，消费必需品股表现疲软，重量级好市多 (Costco) 下跌近 4%，沃尔玛 (Walmart) 下跌约 2.5%。有报道称，特朗普政府希望北美制造的汽车拥有 82% 的地区性成分，以获得美国-墨西哥-加拿大协议规定的优惠待遇，标准普尔汽车制造商指数下跌。通用汽车和美国上市公司 Stellantis 的股价双双收低。

whyItMatters: 这条信息属于财经市场信号，适合用于观察利率、汇率、股债商品、金融机构和宏观政策变化。
